review_text: Très bel endroit avec toilettes impeccables. Les propriétaires sont serviable et accueillants.
Scott is a very nice person, and we love this place, highly recommended!

1. Friendly and accommodating host.
2. Free kayaks and paddle boat for use.
3. Late checkout with no extra fee.
4. My son had no luck catching a fish on his own but the host ensured he did not leave without a catch. Pic below.
5. Great location close to Cradle Mountain.

Just spent 2 nights here. Owners lovely and very helpful. Toilets were spotless. Firewood available to purchase. Sites grassed & free BBQ’s with gas bottles all around. Ample toilet paper. Would highly recommend this beautiful, peaceful place 😊
Loved this campsite - beautiful spot
Keine Dusche nur Toilette dafür 40 Dollar. War der teuerste campground durch unsere Fahrt durch Tassi

Beautiful and peacefull place to camp with boats on free use for campers. The toilets are very clean. The owners are very welcoming and friendly ; my car didn't have battery anymore and the owner helped me with this, thank you again !
You would not find a better place for a trophy fish Scott has done a miracle in developing his fishery. Would highly recommend!!

Linda was so kind and accomodating during my stay. As a solo female traveler she made me feel so safe and welcome and was an all round 5 star host. Such a beautiful setting with lush green grass and close to Cradle. Me and my dog loved it! Big thanks again 🩷-Claudia x
This place is awesome 👌

Brilliant campsite for either before or after Cradle Mountain

Really friendly / nice hosts
Very nice camp ground. Lake, ponds, pukekoes, peacocks

One of our favourite campsites we stayed in during our time in Tassie! We stayed here over new years - the spot was so beautiful, lots of wildlife about and close to cradle mountain for hiking during the day time. The campsite has gas barbecues, a basic sink to wash dishes (they even left out warm soapy water in the evenings), portaloos, fire pits and plenty of firewood you can purchase. They also have fishing rods, kayaks and even a pedal boat - way more on offer than I ever expected. We loved it here.
Great price for a camp spot
Lovely spot to stay if you’re looking to stay near cradle mountain, calm beautiful atmosphere, facilities close and cleaned regularly, spots for tent and camper vans, owners super nice :)

Nice place to camp close to cradle mountain. The lake was nice and facilities were good. WiFi internet included with good range was very nice to have access to at the campsite. Wood for purchase. Good garbage bins and tables available plus fire pits. Access to kayak and paddle boat for nice paddle on the lake. Water accessible but needed to boil first.

Short distance from Cradle Mt as an alternative. Porta loos but no showers. If staying a few nights make sure your water tanks are full. Tried the fishing but no luck. For summer it was cold. Wifi available and phone coverage, weak, at the entrance to the park.

Amazing location. Clean toilets. Multiple species of trout in dam with option to catch and release. Use of paddle boat and kayaks included. Wi-fi. Can have fire drum. Firewood available to purchase. Powered sites available.  Local pademelons and turbo chooks. Multiple bbqs available.
We had an uncovered site for 1 days over the Christmas Holidays, the facility is good, very clean well kept toilets, a nice dam for walking around or fishing.

Large open area, but that results in a lot of wind.
Close to Cradle Mountain.
Portable toilets, but clean and stocked with toilet paper and soap.
Fires allowed in firepit.
No showers.
Overall, not bad a night.
Beautiful camp spot for powered or unpowered, clean toilets, beautiful lake, great hosts. Highly recommend 🌟
It's an open space campground with no tree to break out the strong winds. If you have a normal tent or a rooftop tent, it'll be difficult.

Amazing fishing and camping destination! The property offers excellent value with fantastic amenities including BBQ facilities and well-maintained fire pits. We spotted platypus daily in the lake, along with kangaroos and wallabies. The friendly owners provided knowledgeable tips about kayaking on the dam and walking trails. Clean facilities and reasonable prices make this a must-visit for outdoor enthusiasts seeking wildlife encounters and peaceful lakeside camping.

Fantastic spot! Saw platypus every morning playing in the water, fluffy roos, wallabies, water hens and all sorts of cresture sounds day & night. Ample BBQs provided, clean well stocked functional loos, friendly amd knowledgable owners! Hard pack stone covered large camping areas surround by lush grass and incredible views. Highly recommend!

what a spot! myself & the missus + a mate and his partner wanted somewhere to stay nice and close to cradle mountain for the next days plans. Scott was a great host, greeted us when we arrived and overall very helpful with anything we needed.

Wifi is a great addition after not having phone reception for majority of our trips camping spots, although it was a little patchy it worked awesome whilst connected.

We got there quite late in the day, but Scott was happy to do us a deal with fishing for just an hour because that’s all the light we had left- great fun and caught a couple beautiful fish.

clean toilets, fire-pits all round that you’re allowed to move around, gas barbecues, canoes & a paddle boat on the dam were sick aswell.

all in all for $15pp a night everyone enjoyed their stay & we thought it was good value considering what we paid for 2 other campsites on our trip. (15pp @ lake burbery that had minimal swag friendly spots, & ground was absolute soup everywhere), (13pp @ black river camp which only had drop toilets)

cheers scott!

Really lovely host and camp site. The views are stunning and super wholesome.
Would definitely come back here
Clean and close to the national Park
The place are amazing, and scott it's more amazing than the place
A little paradise to be honest. Would love to go back. Lake, wildlife, fire places, dark sky, relaxation pure. Facilities spotless clean. Top!

Owners were away when we stayed here, but Tim was a fantastic host. Very welcoming and friendly. Was very encouraging about our efforts to try and catch a fish. Due to time constraints, we weren't able to try. Would come back here in a heartbeat. The location is so idyllic and peaceful. The pademelons that graze around the property are so cute.
There are plenty of firepits around and they've managed to ensure that most (if not all) sites have power. Firewood was available to purchase at a great price. Also have port-a-loos there. It was perfect for our caravan. We can't wait to come back!

Absolutely amazing location! Informative & accommodating service!! Highly recommend a visit summer or Winter. It was minus 1 overnight when we stayed, so pack warm! Three portable toilets are available, clean & close to campsites.

Lots of rainbow trout to catch & release. Fishery can provide firewood & fishing gear if needed. We caught lots of fish & created some great memories!

Such an experience for our family!!
Beautiful and restful place and saw so much wildlife. Kangaroos and peacocks come out in the evenings.
We went there in May to camp for a night. the host was very nice and welcoming, and made sure we were prepared for the cold night. The place is lovely and quiet
Cozy place near the Cradle Mluntain. Surrounded by nature.

A couple of weeks ago, we found this beautiful campsite. After a hard day cycling, it was a super spot to relax. The owner was very friendly and helpful. The toilets are the cleanest portaloos we have ever seen. There are firepits (firewood additional cost if needed) and bbq's available. Nice level pitches for tents and those with campers or caravans.
Friendly and good
Amazing campsite. Scott is really friendly and keeps the place spotless. Amazing view of the dam and forest. Peaceful spot in close proximity to Cradle Mountain. Would recommend!

Nice spot with views over 2 small lakes, very friendly host, sites clean and well positioned. Not inexpensive though. Small camp kitchen exposed, but portaloos are kept clean and tidy. We had a powered site, but no water - being self contained is good
Saw a platypus. A bit too crowded for us but good facilities and friendly staff.

This place was great value for a spot only 10 mins drive to Cradle Mountain. Scott is a great host and keeps the place very well maintained. Everything very clean and views of the dam are just lovely. Thanks heaps Scott

Great spot to base yourself as it’s approx 10km from cradle mountain national park. Nice level sites. Power available. Toilets , fit pits and wood available to purchase. You can throw a line in the dam. At a cost. There are plenty for large smart trout in there jumping every morning and arvo. The owner is very friendly and accommodating.enjoyed our 3 days there.

Awesome location and by far the cheapest accommodation In cradle mountain, platypus in the lake and the host offers free kayaks to use. Toilets are portaloos but they are well kept, $20 per person when we stayed in late Feb.

Great campsite.  Quiet, well set up, great location BUT if you are needing a powered site, the sites are set up with power board off a powerboard.   It is ok to just charge your battery but if you want to run any appliances, we had to turn off the fridge and hot water to use an appliance.  When there are 2 or 3 vans it is terrible (especially at night when people are cooking).  If anyone uses an appliance it throws the safety at both powerboards.   We had to reset heaps of times per night.   Not really good enough (and not sure how safe) when they advertise as a powered site.

Lovely site within close proximity to Cradle Mountain National Park. However at $20 per person it is overpriced considering the only facilities are portaloos.  Had the price been more affordable or the facilities been better this place would have 5*.

Spot de camping sympa à 30 min de Cradle Mountain. On y est venu en tente de toit. On a eu bien froid le 15 février 😂
Prévoyez plusieurs couches/ couettes.
Il y a des toilettes, des barbecues, des poubelles, des coins feux + bois à disposition. Il y a un lac juste en face, on en a fait le tour à pied au coucher du soleil. Vous pouvez aussi utiliser les kayak et pêcher dans le lac gratuitement.
Very basic but such a beautiful place to camp. Grass or gravel sites amazing views and toilets only but very clean

A very pretty place, close to Cradle Mountain at half the price of the Cradle Caravan Park. Lovely host, free kayaking in the lake and free gas barbecues, fishing (catch & release) and firewood available at extra cost.
Toilets are porta-loos but they are very clean and well appointed, the entire site is very neat and well kept.
Downsides are not much privacy when it's busy and not a lot of shade areas, showers would be nice but I understand the difficulties and expense of providing them
All around a recommended site if you want something close to Cradle Mountain.
Amazing location when visiting Cradle Mountain. Beautiful scenery and good facilities. Sit outside, enjoy the view over a campfire. Scott was so also so helpful.

Scott is an excellent host. We found his place online and went directly there. Upon arrival, Scott invited us to choose our preferred spot to set up the tent. We stayed for two days, including Christmas Eve, when all the grocery stores were closed. Scott kindly sold us lamb sausages at the same price he bought them for, which was very thoughtful.

Overall, the experience was great. We paid $40 per night for parking and a small tent. The Wi-Fi works well when fewer people are connected, but the signal strength may vary depending on your tent’s location.

Hello there!

We really enjoyed our four nights stay in this place. It was fantastic. Scott and Linda have taken care of the camping site very well and they are very kind and they really push their limits to make sure you enjoy your time there. Every single detail is top notch. Definitely make sure to pick this camping spot, you won’t regret it. Highly recommended 😊

Thanks a ton Scott & Linda!

Raja, Mandira, Aagya & Enya
XX

Scott was a legend of a host and what a perfect spot, overlooking the large dam, kids loved canoeing and it was so fresh for a dip after hiking all day. Everything was clean and looked after. Fishing for rainbow trout was super fun too! We even saw the cutest echidna out front of our camp. Highly recommend ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Convenient location 10mins drive from Cradle Mountain visitor centre. Beautiful lakefront view, and very clean portaloos. Note that there’s no showers. Free wifi available. Lots of fun activities like free kayaks, fire pits (you pay for the firewood) and fishing (at extra cost) although we didn’t get a chance to try these due to rain.

Beautiful camp site. There are fire pits, free barbecues, and lots of sites around the reservoir. Ground was a bit hard and rocky so bring a sleeping mat if you have one. Kayaks to use on the water and a lovely walk in the back forest. Super clean portaloo toilets.

I saw three platypus up at the same time and there was a sea eagle hanging around too! Very special spot to camp.

Beautiful camp site and super nice owner!! Got to go canoeing and enjoy the beautiful lake. Toilets are also super clean and bbq are there to use. Super close to the cradle mountain information centre. Would highly recommend!!
Really a great spot. Finishing is fun and amazing views
Great little stop over .

A really nice camping spot near Cradle Mountain (15 mins drive to the visitor centre). Scott was welcoming and friendly without being obtrusive. He gave us fish feed to feed the fish as well, which was nice of him. Campsite was flat and really well designed, plus there were plenty of wallabies to see!

We booked directly instead of on HipCamp because it was cheaper and Scott gets all of the money, and it was $50, which we thought was reasonable considering the area and the fact it was powered. He also filled up our van with water for free and offered us some books to read. Very cute dog Ruby as well!

Thanks Scott!
What a great place to go camping! The hosts where great and very accomodating. We cought 6 trouts while fishing there and had a very fun time!
Tim was an amazing host!
The campsite scenery is superb. The firepot and firewoods burn well. The night sky is magical. The host is super supportive!

Great spot of respite if you're looking to hike cradle mountain. Very chilled, calm and relaxing spot by the dam. The owners were so friendly and helpful. Firewood was $20 and had the fire starters, kindling then big wood (they've thought everything through here!)

We even saw a platypus swimming around at dusk for 10-15 mins looking for dinner, was magical. Would highly recommend staying here.

**WIFI does not work, consistently, if at all. Someone camped next to us had to set up their own starlink for access. Even with telstra as my provider- not logged into their WiFi- I had limited and sketchy phone and internet reception. (I note this incase someone else, like me, had planned a couple of days RNR here to catch up on work/other commitments or further plan their journey, and call family/friends). Because I found that particularly frustrating.

**FISHING is not inclusive with camp costs. It is $50per person per day. And is catch and release, which is cool, if you know prior to booking. I didn't. And I couldn't justify an extra $50/$100 for myself or my daughter to have a go.

Besides these two issues...we had a lovely few days. Level sites. Clean portaloos. Fun kayaking and walking around the dam. Quiet place to stay.

Amazing facilities
Great lake walk

Wow! What an amazing experience, we caught and released 2 trout and had so much fun! The host was so lovely and helpful, the view was amazing from the camp spots but we just fished.
We could not rate this any higher! You pay a small fee and he sets you up with all the fishing gear you need. Loved out time there! Thankyou!
Cher ! 40 dollars la nuit pour juste un emplacement et des toilettes. On a regretté de ne pas avoir dormi sur le parking du Visitor Center
I lovely spot. Scott is very friendly. Fun to kayak in the dams and walk around. Great location.

Wonderful property and super chill vibe. Scott is very customer oriented and super easy going. Highly recommend this place for camping and relaxing and as a base of operations for visiting Cradle Mountain National Park.
We had an amazing time here. The kids loved feeding the fishes and the complimentary kayaking!

This campsite is beautiful, locates very close to Cradle Mountain. It has a small pond where guests can do a little kayaking or walking around the area. The owners are nice people, they also provide the guests firepits, firewood (have to pay for it), 3 clean toilets, and a dishwashing sink. Will camp again when we have a chance.
$22.50 per person per night unpowered no water to fill van tanks.Portable toilets (there was no soap when we stayed). Kayak and fishing rods free to use. 10mins from Cradle mountain.
Fantastic location for camping and caravan/RV stays. Peaceful, well set up, good facilities available and well priced. Owner was very welcoming and helpful. Would definitely stay here again.
Thank you very much for the wonderful experience here. Our kids really loved this place! 10 mins drive to Cradle Mountain. So easy to get too, would definitely stay again!

We had an amazing two nights at Cradle Mountain Fishery and Camping. Beautiful location, close to Cradle Mountain. Clean toilets & water & firewood available too. Friendly, helpful staff and all in all a fabulous place to camp with family
& friends. Highly recommended

We stayed here for one night over the weekend whilst we spent two days exploring the national park and couldn't have been more content! We called late in the evening and Scott was super accomodating - it wasn't too busy and there was a spot for our camper right by the dam!

So much wildlife on the property - we spotted platypus, water rats, and so much beautiful bird life! The toilets were super clean, and there were even kayaks we could use. Would not hesitate to come back when I next visit the park!

We can't asked for more as we arrived late and left early for Cradle mountain. Scott is an A+ host who assisted us after 9pm and before 7am with most the campervan set up. All we needed is clean toilet, water dump, fresh water top up and power - all checked here :)

Cheers,

Milo dynosawrr peeps/campervan noobs.

We spent an overnighter here whilst doing some walking trails at Cradle Mountain, very economical price for a powered site as we had a compact motor home.
Scenic place to stay and hosts Scott & Linda are extremely helpful and friendly. Best part of the stay was the awesome fishing my husband had, Scott was knowledgeable and friendly and my husbands catch made his stay!
Thanks again for the memories!! Shane & Bron

Just spent 4 days here. Had a wonderful time looking around the area, watching wombats in the wild. But the best thing was catching 3 trout. 2 keepers and one returned, to keep on swimming. My first time trout fishing. Scott was very welcoming and gave me fishing tips. The set up is very relaxing.And dog friendly, Spotless port a loos. Will definitely be back when weather is warmer.
